details: The supplied article_text contains the quoted compound 2b characterization with m.p. >300 °C; DOI, value, relation, measured data_type, and compound binding all match.
The source quote is present for compound 3i, but the paper prints the melting point as the shorthand '233-35°C', while value_raw is recorded as '233-235 °C' rather than the printed raw value.
The HTML article text contains the 1a heading and visible characterization text 'White powder, 81%. mp 255 °C'; DOI, measured data_type, value, and compound binding match.
The article_text section 3.12 contains compound 2e with 'mp 123.5-157.2 °C'; DOI, midpoint/range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The quote and surrounding section are for chlorido-[1,3-diethyl-4-anisyl-5-(4-chlorophenyl)imidazole-2-ylidene]gold(I) tetrafluoroborate (11), not a platinum(II) N-heterocyclic carbene complex.
The PDF text on p. 2497 contains the compound 22 procedure and 'solid (mp 179-183 °C'; the range midpoint, measured data_type, legacy source citation, and compound binding are supported.
Table 1 in article_text contains row 6 with the named methyl ester and melting point 152-154; DOI, range midpoint, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The source compound 6 name includes the leading terpenyl substituent '2-[(1'R,6'R)-6'-Isopropenyl-3'-methylcyclohex-2-en-1-yl]-4-', which is omitted from the row compound_name.
Table 5 contains Baricitinib Tm [K] literature value 487.15 under the [47] column; 487.15 K - 273.15 = 214.00 °C, and the compiled measured data_type is supported.
Using the specified paper_path HTML file, the claimed Ag(AN)2(BF4) quote and 132 °C value are not present; the HTML is a Europe PMC shell rather than the article text.
The quoted 117-119 °C passage belongs to section 4.1.2, 1-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-2-(3-nitro-1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)ethanone (5), not 4-(2-nitro-1H-imidazol-1-yl)benzaldehyde.
The paper lists compound 26a as (2S,4R,E)-..., while the row names (2R,4R,E)-... (26a); the (2R,4R,E) stereoisomer is compound 26b, so the code/stereochemistry binding is wrong.
The article_text characterization for compound 5a contains 'M.p. 97-98 °C'; DOI, value range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text characterization for Isopropyle 1-hydroxy-1H-indole-2-carboxylate 2e contains 'mp 150-152 °C'; DOI, value, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text B5 characterization contains 'Melting point: 93-94 °C'; DOI, value range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text section 3.2.9 contains (E)-6-(1-Pentenyl)salicylic acid (9a) with 'Mp 96-98 °C'; DOI, value range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The source maps compound IV to bis(2-nitrobenzylidene)-1,3-diaminopropane and Table 1 gives M.P 68-70; DOI, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
Using the specified paper_path HTML file, the claimed Ag(GN)2(BF4) quote and 147 °C value are not present; the HTML is a Europe PMC shell rather than the article text.
The raw HTML contains the claimed contiguous snippet including compound 3b and 'Mp: 207-210 °C'; DOI, range midpoint, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text section 3.2.13 contains compound 5j with 'Melting Point: 291-292 °C'; DOI, value range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text contains '1-Indanone : mp 40°C'; DOI, exact value, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text characterization for compound 11c contains 'mp: 234-236 °C'; DOI, value range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text characterization for compound 11a contains 'mp: 250-252 °C'; DOI, value range, measured data_type, and compound binding match.
The article_text characterization for compound 4b contains 'mp >300°C (decomp.)'; DOI, decomposition property, relation, measured data_type, and compound binding match.

The quoted boiling point is for the 'desired aldimine' prepared from cyclohexylamine and butyraldehyde; the row compound_name N-benzylidene-2-ethylcyclohexylamine is not supported by the source context.
